anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Living traditions that safely transmit difficult words through institution, ritual, teacher relationship, and community without asking each receiver to analyze wounds.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Confidence remains moderate. Receiver-wound coding should be a risk triage for extracted, online, therapeutic, or self-synthesis contexts, not a universal condition for all religious language.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If a novice learns detachment, surrender, emptiness, or service through embodied correction rather than private receiver-wound reflection, the proposed sheet may be an outsider's psychologizing grid. It could weaken trust, increase self-monitoring, or miss the actual correction holder.

claim_units: A spiritual term is not portable by dictionary meaning alone.
Terms carry training practices, correction authorities, verification rules, and forms of conduct.
A modern receiver's loneliness, shame, ambition, fear, or visibility hunger can counterfeit the term's use.
Detachment, surrender, emptiness, witness, humility, and service can become withdrawal, passivity, nihilism, self-erasure, or performance identity.

Human-condition fit is clearer than in many comparison claims: it targets loneliness, digital comparison, withdrawal, meaning loss, and achievement-contingent self-worth in stable supported readers, not acute crisis.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: When these words are imported without trained practice and correction holders, different wounds will counterfeit them predictably: boundaries becomes avoidance, resilience becomes overwork, safety becomes control, accountability becomes shame ritual.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Terms such as boundaries, resilience, trauma-informed, accountability, psychological safety, and self-care also travel with practices, authorities, verification norms, and receiver wounds.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Definitions alone transfer the terms safely across cohorts, and misuse does not vary by receiver wound, correction holder, or institutional practice.

exact_claim: A spiritual word travels with practice, authority, verification, and the receiver's wound; therefore translation-strain work must test both source-side role and receiver-side misuse before a borrowed term becomes a responsible teaching.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Detachment skews toward withdrawal in lonely or avoidant participants, but toward disciplined non-reactivity in supported overattached participants. Service skews toward self-erasure in achievement-contingent participants, but toward concrete repair in stable participants.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Receiver wounds create predictable distortions of the same borrowed term.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Pre-registered cohort study coding wound profile, term use, action taken, and follow-up conduct.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Term misuse is not predicted by receiver wound after controlling for teacher quality, community support, mental health, and prior practice.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Compared with ordinary journaling, stable users produce more concrete accountable actions, fewer avoidance rationalizations, and no increase in shame or rumination.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The Word Carry Test improves conduct more than ordinary reflection for the target cohort.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Randomized pilot with three arms: Word Carry Test, ordinary journaling, and action-plan control.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Ordinary journaling, values clarification, or a simple action plan performs as well or better, or the practice increases checking, shame, or self-surveillance.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Teachers and spiritual directors name different warnings for the same word depending on loneliness, shame, ambition, trauma history, social support, and online visibility pressure.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Source role plus receiver wound predicts practitioner warnings better than source role alone.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Interview study asking for concrete misuse and repair cases, not agreement with the theory.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Practitioners say tradition, teacher style, social ecology, and clinical risk fully explain the warning profile without a receiver-wound field.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Public posts using terms such as surrender, emptiness, witness, boundaries, service, or humility without practice source or correction holder show higher rates of avoidance, grandiosity, withdrawal, or performance identity markers.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Online extraction intensifies word misuse when correction holders are absent.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Corpus coding of spiritual, therapeutic, and self-help social media posts with blinded behavioral-risk tags.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Misuse rates are unrelated to correction-holder absence or are better predicted only by platform incentives and mental-health variables.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate turns this into a teaching gate for spiritual borrowing and adds receiver-wound risk.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade for the word-does-not-travel-alone thesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Meaning is bound to use, rules, activity, and forms of life rather than words alone.
near_neighbors.source: Wittgenstein on language-games and forms of life, SEP, https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/fall2023/entries/wittgenstein/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds modern psychological and digital-comparison misuse as a receiver-side field.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrade to extended. The authority and practice side is established prior art.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close. Asad already ties religious discourse to correct form and purpose of practice, history, institutions, and social conditions.
near_neighbors.source: Talal Asad on discursive tradition, summarized with source citation at https://www.scielo.org.za/scielo.php?pid=S1011-76012014000200004&script=sci_arttext

near_neighbors.difference: Panikkar focuses on interreligious correspondence; the candidate adds cohort harm and action testing.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on functional analogy that preserves specificity and avoids mistaken comparison.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate makes performative role a safety check for borrowed words in modern practice design.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Reduces novelty of the words-do-things component.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. Religious utterances can be performative, imperative, authority-establishing, vow-making, or conduct-shaping rather than merely descriptive.
near_neighbors.source: Religious-language and speech-act approaches, summarized at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Problem_of_religious_language

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate joins bypass risk to source-side translation strain and cross-tradition teaching gates.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major downgrade for the receiver-wound insight, moderate support for novel synthesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close on receiver wounds: spiritual ideas and practices can be used to avoid unresolved emotional issues, psychological wounds, responsibility, and developmental tasks.
near_neighbors.source: Spiritual bypassing literature, overview at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spiritual_bypass, and clinical citations listed there.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate applies contextual-risk logic to individual borrowed terms rather than meditation experiences generally.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Keeps the model empirical-adjacent but lowers novelty.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close. VCE shows similar experiences can be interpreted and managed differently by practitioner factors, practice factors, relationships, health behaviors, and worldviews.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., Varieties of Contemplative Experience, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239, and Palitsky, Lindahl et al. on worldviews, https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/13634615221128679

near_neighbors.difference: This record attaches that rule specifically to translation strain and borrowed vocabulary.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrade from candidate discovery to extended.
near_neighbors.overlap: Near exact local prior on testing sentence or word role before trusting a spiritual claim, plus naming the modern wound before teaching.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary, First Ask What the Words Do and modern-human-condition gating from project memory.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a standalone discovery. Cross-index with First Ask What the Words Do, translation-strain records, spiritual-bypass safety records, and modern-human-condition gating.

Build a one-page codebook with fields for source role, trained practice, correction holder, verification rule, receiver wound, likely counterfeit use, concrete action, warning, repair, and clinical exclusion.
Use split-source coding: code source role from primary instruction texts, then predict misuse and repair from held-out practitioner cases.
Compare the model against spiritual bypassing, Pargament religious coping, VCE contextual factors, Asad discursive tradition, Lindbeck grammar, and ordinary values-based action planning.
Pilot the Word Carry Test only with stable supported adults or older teens, and stop for shame, panic, rumination, dissociation, compulsive confession, self-punishment, or obsessive checking.
Require a real community or care pathway for lonely seekers, since the practice can remove false comfort without meeting the belonging need.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.62 to 0.39. The core insight is strongly anticipated by Wittgenstein, Asad, Lindbeck, speech-act theory, Panikkar, spiritual bypassing, Pargament, and VCE context research. The useful Lumenary contribution is a practical two-sided gate for source role and receiver-wound misuse before public teaching. Human-condition fit is stronger than novelty: the target is stable supported readers facing loneliness, digital comparison, withdrawal, meaning loss, and achievement-contingent self-worth. It is not fit for acute crisis, unsafe relationships, psychosis, mania, severe depression, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, OCD or scrupulosity loops, or medical blackout concerns.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is simply spiritual bypassing, language-games, Asad, Lindbeck, or ordinary pastoral discernment, and that the added worksheet gives no better repair than existing guidance.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners identify cases where source role plus receiver-wound coding predicts specific warnings and repairs better than tradition label, teacher style, or generic bypass language.
questions: When this word fails in students, what concrete behavior changes first?
Who normally corrects misuse of this word: teacher, text, ritual, community, vow, clinician, ordinary duty, or the practitioner?
Does naming a receiver wound improve repair, or does it overpsychologize a tradition-bound term?
Can you name a case where detachment, surrender, emptiness, witness, humility, or service became avoidance, passivity, nihilism, self-erasure, or performance identity?
Does the Word Carry Test change practice understanding beyond standard spiritual-bypass screening and ordinary teacher guidance?
relevant_practitioners: clinicians familiar with spiritual bypassing and meditation-related difficulties
teachers working with online seekers and deconstructed former believers

primary_pair: Daodejing 1, https://daoreading.com/ddj_ch_1, read against Pseudo-Dionysius, Mystical Theology, via accessible summary and source links, https://saintquote.org/work/mystical_theology/
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The model risks turning every translation problem into private psychology. In many traditions, institutions, teachers, liturgies, vows, and communities carry correction more strongly than an individual's wound analysis. The Word Carry Test may increase shame, rumination, self-surveillance, or dependence if used by people with OCD, scrupulosity, trauma activation, mania, severe depression, dissociation, acute addiction withdrawal, unsafe relationships, or clinical distress.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Before raising novelty, read Lindbeck, The Nature of Doctrine, Talal Asad, The Idea of an Anthropology of Islam and Genealogies of Religion, Wittgenstein on language-games and forms of life, J. L. Austin on speech acts, MacIntyre on practices, Bourdieu on habitus, Panikkar on homeomorphic equivalence, Katz and Sharf on contextualism, McMahan on Buddhist modernism, Pargament on religious coping, spiritual bypassing research by Welwood, Cashwell, Fox, Picciotto, and Lindahl and Palitsky on VCE and worldview effects.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23 makes the unseen seer and inner controller the authority-bearing self, while SN 22.59 applies not-self analysis to the aggregates, including consciousness, through affliction and non-mastery. Both de-objectify ordinary identity, but they train opposite uses of the word self. Daodejing 1 restrains naming around the constant way, while Dionysian unknowing is a Christian apophatic ascent toward God beyond affirmation and negation. The shared vocabulary of silence or unknowing is therefore not a stable portable insight unless practice, authority, and verification are carried with it.

title: No Word Travels Alone
unlike_statement: Unlike Wittgenstein, Asad, Lindbeck, speech-act theory, spiritual bypassing, and VCE context research, this candidate says a borrowed spiritual term must pass a two-sided gate: source role plus receiver-wound misuse. That combination is useful for Lumenary practice design, but most parts are known.
